notes: Game run's great Out-of-Box. But disabling esync, the game seems to run smoother.
title: Far Cry® New Dawn

notes:
The game performs great, with a few hickups: 

Steam crashes if I play Far Cry, quit, and then try to play again later, but just restarting steam works fine. 

The other issue is that I cannot steer any vehicle left or right while driving. Most of the time this is not a huge deal as there is an autodrive functionality, but there is one mission that requires driving which was a pain to complete. If I hold a or d while getting into a vehicle it will continuously steer left or right, enabling you to get the angle you need and then exit and reenter the vehicle to go straight forwards. Hugely annoying for the one mission so far where it was required, but the rest of the game works perfectly.
title: Far Cry® New Dawn

notes: Posting this report because a previous report states that it works out of the box at silver; I have tried no tweaks, but can safely say that it does not work out of the box on my system.
title: Far Cry® New Dawn

notes: 16 hours in. Game runs very well so far, except steering left or right in vehicles doesn't work. You can use the auto-drive function or be a passenger and let your companion drive. However, I've encountered 2 missions so far, where you actually have to drive. One of them is somewhat doable, although very painfully. The other one seems to be impossible this way. I've recorded a gameplay snippet: https://youtu.be/vcKoG2FZ9nU
title: Far Cry® New Dawn

notes:
Gotta disable uplay overlay and close the uplay window which appears after about 3mins ingame, otherwise getting horrible mouse stutters. Also had to replace the dinput8.dll with the windows 7 one, otherwise could not steer vehicles.
Getting the same problem as in FC5 with steam reinstalling .Net 3.5 every time.
Everything else works ootb and sets itself up.
title: Far Cry® New Dawn

cpu: AMD Ryzen 3 2200G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes: Ran perfectly the first time.  upon loading the game again,  everything is white and practically unplayable.  fixed by starting a new save(which no one wants to do)
title: Far Cry® New Dawn

notes: I own this game on Uplay but added it as a non-steam game and played it entirely with Proton through Steam. Plays very well on the highest settings, usually getting around 90 fps. Disabled uplay overlay and I copied dinput.dll and dinput8.dll from Windows 8.1 and added the overrides in winecfg.
title: Far Cry® New Dawn

notes: Installed, and was surprised it ran since I hadn't been able to get it to before.  I think Ubisoft updated their launcher because the default settings already had its overlay disabled.  Two big issues everyone has had before still seem present:  the mouse input in-game is really twitchy and seems to "skip," and vehicles can't be steered at all.  I fixed the mouse lag/skipping issue by using winetricks to install dinput8.dll and then setting it to native,builtin with winecfg in the game's wineprefix.  Although this made the mouse input in the game work as smoothly as it should, it did not fix vehicle steering for me.  I don't use Windows anymore so don't have access to a dinput8.dll from Windows 7 to try it with instead, if it's different from the one winetricks downloads.
title: Far Cry® New Dawn
